title: Luminous Vanilla (EMISSIVE)
description: Luminous Vanilla — Emissive Ores, Sculk, Nether & More (All-in-One Pack)
body:
## Luminous Vanilla

**Luminous Vanilla** is a complete emissive resource pack designed to enhance Minecraft’s atmosphere without altering its vanilla feel. It combines emissive textures for ores, Sculk blocks, Nether blocks, Amethyst blocks, and more, creating a seamless experience.

If you want your world to feel more alive at night, more readable in caves, and more visually striking without changing the game's core style, this pack is built for that.

---

## ✨ What This Pack Does

**Luminous Vanilla** adds carefully designed emissive textures to a wide range of blocks across the Overworld, the Nether, and the Deep Dark. These emissive elements glow in darkness, making exploration smoother and environments more immersive, all while staying true to Minecraft’s original aesthetic.

This is not a random glow effect slapped onto textures. Every block has been adjusted with intention, balancing visibility, style, and consistency.

---

## 🎯 Why Choose Luminous Vanilla?

### ✅ All-in-One Solution

No more combining 5–10 different emissive packs.

Everything is unified, consistent, and designed to work together.

---

### ✅ Vanilla-Friendly

The goal is enhancement, not transformation.

* No extreme brightness
* No clashing styles
* No “modded” look

It still feels like Minecraft, just better.

---

### 🚀 Performance Friendly

This is a lightweight resource pack:

* No heavy shaders required
* No unnecessary complexity
* Works smoothly even on mid-range systems
* It's only 182 kb in size. 😭🙏

---

## 🧩 Compatibility & Requirements

To see emissive textures properly, you will need one of the following:

* _[Continuity](https://modrinth.com/mod/continuity)_
   **or**
* _OptiFine_

_(I recommend [Continuity](https://modrinth.com/mod/continuity))_           
These tools allow Minecraft to render emissive textures correctly.

---

## 🖼️ Visual Showcase

This pack is best experienced visually. It shines in:

* Dark caves glow with ores.
* Ancient Cities shimmer with enhanced sculk atmosphere.
* Nether biomes blaze with glowing vegetation.
* Amethyst geodes radiate soft light.

For best results, combine with shaders, but it also looks great in vanilla lighting.

---

## 🔄 Version Support

**Luminous Vanilla** is actively maintained and supports multiple Minecraft versions, each properly configured for:

* Compatibility
* Stability
* Correct pack format usage

---

## ⌛ Coming Soon!

Planned features & improvements:

* [Respackopts](https://modrinth.com/mod/respackopts) support
* More optional variants
* Additional emissive blocks & mobs
  
<details>
<summary>More emissive textures:</summary>

- Enchanting Table
- Brewing Stand
- Chorus Flower
- Copper Bulbs
- **Trial Spawners & Vaults**
- Raw Iron & Gold blocks
- Glow Lichen
- Magma Block
- **Crying Obsidian**
- **Respawn Anchor**
- **Resin Clump**
- Crimson & Warped Signs
- See Pickle
- **Dragon Egg**
- **End Portal**
- **Nether Portal**
- More Redstone stuff
<details>
<summary>Emissive entities:</summary>

- **Ender Dragon**
- **Warden**
- **Creaking**
- End Crystal
- Endermite
- **Glow Squid**
- **Drowned**
- Skeleton, Parched & Bogged
- Ender Chest
- Trapped Chest
- Trident


</details>


<details>
<summary>Upcoming improvements & fixes:</summary>

- **Making blue Ancient Debris emissive**
- Calibrated Sculk Sensor top fix
- Better mob **Glow** effect
- **Adding amethyst & sculk block variations so not all amethyst & sculk blocks become emissive, as it can be eye straining asf.** This way, every 1 in 3 blocks will be emissive.


</details>

_Items in **bold** are the top priority at the moment._

</details>



---

## 💡 Final Thoughts:

**Luminous Vanilla** was created with a simple goal:

> Make Minecraft glow without ruining what makes it Minecraft.

If you’re tired of managing multiple emissive packs or dealing with inconsistent visuals, this pack offers a clean, polished solution.

Whether you’re exploring caves, building in the Nether, or walking through the Deep Dark, **Luminous Vanilla** enhances every moment with subtle, immersive lighting.

---

<details>
<summary>Don't want certain blocks being emissive?</summary>

**If you don't like certain blocks being emissive, for example coal ore, then you can open up the resource pack and delete the emissive texture for that ore. I personally remove coal ore as it can look brown when emissive with shaders turned on. Here's an example:**

- Navigate to "assets/minecraft/textures/block"
- Delete coal_ore_e (emissive textures end with "_e")
- And you're done!

</details>

If you ever encounter any issues or bugs you can always report it on my [github](https://github.com/rdq-09/Luminous-Vanilla/issues).

**Check "[Gallery](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/luminous-vanilla/gallery)" too**

**Give it a try and see your world in a new light. I hope you like it! :D**
